mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with rate cuts over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer and business demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in retail sales and housing starts fails to predict positive quarterly returns (out-of-sample accuracy < 55%).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next week.
failure_condition: Industrial production gains do not translate to positive 5-day returns during early-cycle phases (t-stat < 1.5 over 5-year sample).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating monthly inflation erodes real corporate margins and prompts expectations of tighter monetary policy, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: High inflation months consistently coincide with positive 20-day equity returns, contradicting margin compression expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payrolls growth combined with stable or declining unemployment confirms robust labor market health, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal loses predictive power when unemployment is already below 3.5% (Rank IC drops below 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects sustained economic expansion and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP revisions show zero or negative correlation with subsequent 60-day equity returns across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in industrial production signals immediate supply chain disruptions or demand contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Sharp industrial production declines are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 60% of cases, indicating market decoupling from supply shocks.
